./.title: Preface
./.summary: A light library including a collection of frontend utility functions used in daily work
./.body:
Light 4kb-after-gzipped library including a collection of frontend utility functions used in daily work


  
    
  
  
    
  
  
    
  



A simple yet useful frontend utility library for use in daily work. If you find it useful, you can fork it and change the content until it meet the requirements in your daily work.


🔥 Detailed online documentation: utils-daily
🔥 4kb after gzipped (use rollup.js with the technology called tree-shaking)
🔥 All browsers supported
🔥 Use flow to do the type check work
🔥 Use eslint to lint code
🔥 Use jasmine to do unit test

Usage
Via script tag in html


  console.log(u.getType('')) // 'string'


Via npm
First, install it:
npm i -S utils-daily

Then import it:
// import while library and use the method you like
import u from 'utils-daily'
u.getType(123) // 'number'

// or if you only want to import specified function
import getType from 'utils-daily/getType'
getType(123) // 'number'

Open Source
Source code is hoisted in Github: Yakima-Teng/utils-daily.
Pull requests are welcome ^_^.
License
MIT

common/hasValue.html.title: hasValue
common/hasValue.html.body:
hasValue(val) ⇒ boolean
Judge whether a variable is valuable

''、null、'null'、undefined、'undefined' is regarded as invaluable
number 0 is regarded as valuable

Kind: global functionReturns: boolean - whether a variable is valuable  



Param
Description




val
variable



Example  
console.log(hasValue('')) // false
console.log(hasValue(null) // false
console.log(hasValue('null') // false
console.log(hasValue(undefined) // false
console.log(hasValue('undefined') // false
console.log(hasValue(0) // true
console.log(hasValue('none') // true


© 2018 Yakima Teng @ https://github.com/Yakima-Teng

common/getType.html.title: getType
common/getType.html.body:
getType(val) ⇒ string
Get the type of a variable
Kind: global functionReturns: string - 'array', 'object', 'function', 'null', 'undefined', 'string', 'number', 'boolean', 'date', 'regexp' and etc.  



Param
Type
Description




val
any
the variable



Example  
console.log(getType({}) // 'object'
console.log(getType([]) // 'array'
console.log(getType(() => {})) // 'function'
console.log(getType(null)) // 'null'
console.log(getType(undefined)) // 'undefined'
console.log(getType('')) // 'string'
console.log(getType(123)) // 'number'
console.log(getType(true)) // 'boolean'
console.log(getType(new Date())) // 'date'
console.log(getType(/^[0-9]{3}/)) // 'regexp'
console.log(getType('test')) // 'string'


© 2018 Yakima Teng @ https://github.com/Yakima-Teng

common/debounce.html.title: debounce
common/debounce.html.body:
debounce(func, [wait], [options]) ⇒ function
It's lodash.debounce function.
Creates a debounced function that delays invoking func until after wait
milliseconds have elapsed since the last time the debounced function was
invoked. The debounced function comes with a cancel method to cancel
delayed func invocations and a flush method to immediately invoke them.
Provide options to indicate whether func should be invoked on the
leading and/or trailing edge of the wait timeout. The func is invoked
with the last arguments provided to the debounced function. Subsequent
calls to the debounced function return the result of the last func
invocation.
Note: If leading and trailing options are true, func is
invoked on the trailing edge of the timeout only if the debounced function
is invoked more than once during the wait timeout.
If wait is 0 and leading is false, func invocation is deferred
until to the next tick, similar to setTimeout with a timeout of 0.
See David Corbacho's article
for details over the differences between _.debounce and _.throttle.
Kind: global functionReturns: function - Returns the new debounced function.  



Param
Type
Default
Description




func
function

The function to debounce.


[wait]
number
0
The number of milliseconds to delay.


[options]
Object
{}
The options object.


[options.leading]
boolean
false
Specify invoking on the leading edge of the timeout.


[options.maxWait]
number

The maximum time func is allowed to be delayed before it's invoked.


[options.trailing]
boolean
true
Specify invoking on the trailing edge of the timeout.



Example  
// Avoid costly calculations while the window size is in flux.
jQuery(window).on('resize', _.debounce(calculateLayout, 150));

// Invoke `sendMail` when clicked, debouncing subsequent calls.
jQuery(element).on('click', _.debounce(sendMail, 300, {
  'leading': true,
  'trailing': false
}));

// Ensure `batchLog` is invoked once after 1 second of debounced calls.
var debounced = _.debounce(batchLog, 250, { 'maxWait': 1000 });
var source = new EventSource('/stream');
jQuery(source).on('message', debounced);

// Cancel the trailing debounced invocation.
jQuery(window).on('popstate', debounced.cancel);


© 2018 Yakima Teng @ https://github.com/Yakima-Teng

common/throttle.html.title: throttle
common/throttle.html.body:
throttle(func, [wait], [options]) ⇒ function
It's lodash.throttle function.
Creates a throttled function that only invokes func at most once per
every wait milliseconds. The throttled function comes with a cancel
method to cancel delayed func invocations and a flush method to
immediately invoke them. Provide options to indicate whether func
should be invoked on the leading and/or trailing edge of the wait
timeout. The func is invoked with the last arguments provided to the
throttled function. Subsequent calls to the throttled function return the
result of the last func invocation.
Note: If leading and trailing options are true, func is
invoked on the trailing edge of the timeout only if the throttled function
is invoked more than once during the wait timeout.
If wait is 0 and leading is false, func invocation is deferred
until to the next tick, similar to setTimeout with a timeout of 0.
See David Corbacho's article
for details over the differences between _.throttle and _.debounce.
Kind: global functionReturns: function - Returns the new throttled function.  



Param
Type
Default
Description




func
function

The function to throttle.


[wait]
number
0
The number of milliseconds to throttle invocations to.


[options]
Object
{}
The options object.


[options.leading]
boolean
true
Specify invoking on the leading edge of the timeout.


[options.trailing]
boolean
true
Specify invoking on the trailing edge of the timeout.



Example  
// Avoid excessively updating the position while scrolling.
jQuery(window).on('scroll', _.throttle(updatePosition, 100));

// Invoke `renewToken` when the click event is fired, but not more than once every 5 minutes.
var throttled = _.throttle(renewToken, 300000, { 'trailing': false });
jQuery(element).on('click', throttled);

// Cancel the trailing throttled invocation.
jQuery(window).on('popstate', throttled.cancel);


© 2018 Yakima Teng @ https://github.com/Yakima-Teng

object/extend.html.title: extend
object/extend.html.body:
extend([deep], target, object1, [objectN]) ⇒ Object
Merge the contents of two or more objects together into the first object.

This is the jQuery.extend method

Kind: global functionReturns: Object - the modified target object  



Param
Type
Description




[deep]
boolean
Optional; if true, the merge becomes recursive (aka. deep copy). Passing false for this argument is not supported.


target
Object
The object to extend. It will receive the new properties.


object1
Object
An object containing additional properties to merge in.


[objectN]
Object
Additional objects containing properties to merge in.



Example  
const objA = {
  a: '1',
  b: ['1', '2', '3'],
  c: { d: 'e' }
}
const objB = {
  a: 'e'
}
extend({}, objA, objB, { a: objB }) // => { a: { a: 'e' }, b: ['1', '2', '3'], c: { d: 'e' } }
console.log(objA.a === objB) // true
const objC = extend(true, {}, { a: objA })
console.log(objC.a === objA) // false
// objA will be changed
extend(objA, { b: null }) // => { a: '1', b: null, c: { d: 'e' } }


© 2018 Yakima Teng @ https://github.com/Yakima-Teng

object/assign.html.title: assign
object/assign.html.body:
assign(target, sources) ⇒ Object
Copy the values of all enumerable own properties from one or more source objects to a target object
Kind: global functionReturns: Object - the target Object  



Param
Type
Description




target
Object
the target object


sources
Array.
the source object(s)



Example  
const objA = { a: 1 }
assign({}, objA) // { a: 1 }
// objA will be changed
assign(objA, { b: 2 }) // { a: 1, b: 2 }


© 2018 Yakima Teng @ https://github.com/Yakima-Teng

object/deepClone.html.title: deepClone
object/deepClone.html.body:
deepClone(obj) ⇒ Object
Clone target object deeply

The same as extend(true, {}, obj)

Kind: global functionReturns: Object - a new object cloned from target object, but is not the target object  



Param
Type
Description




obj
Object
the target object



Example  
const objA = {
  a: '1',
  b: ['1', '2', '3'],
  c: { d: 'e' }
}
const objB = {
  a: 'e'
}
const objC = deepClone({}, objA, objB, { a: objB }) // { a: { a: 'e' }, b: ['1', '2', '3'], c: { d: 'e' } }
console.log(objC.a === objB) // false


© 2018 Yakima Teng @ https://github.com/Yakima-Teng

object/shallowClone.html.title: shallowClone
object/shallowClone.html.body:
shallowClone(obj) ⇒ Object
Clone target object shallowly

The same as extend({}, obj)

Kind: global functionReturns: Object - a new object cloned from target object, but is not the target object  



Param
Type
Description




obj
Object
the target object



Example  
const objA = {
  a: '1',
  b: ['1', '2', '3'],
  c: { d: 'e' }
}
const objB = {
  a: 'e'
}
const objC = shallowClone({}, objA, objB, { a: objB }) // { a: { a: 'e' }, b: ['1', '2', '3'], c: { d: 'e' } }
console.log(objC.a === objB) // true


© 2018 Yakima Teng @ https://github.com/Yakima-Teng

date/getRelativeDateString.html.title: getRelativeDateString
date/getRelativeDateString.html.body:
getRelativeDateString(dat, yDiff, mDiff, dDiff, [hDiff], [miDiff], [sDiff]) ⇒ string
Get date string of calculating specific date with specific difference in year, month, date, hours, minutes and seconds
Kind: global functionReturns: string - date string in format of yyyy-mm-dd or yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss  



Param
Type
Description




dat
Date
date object used as the base for calculation


yDiff
number
difference in year, negative value is acceptable


mDiff
number
difference in month, negative value is acceptable


dDiff
number
difference in date, negative value is acceptable


[hDiff]
number
difference in hours, negative value is acceptable; optional, but if defined, miDiff and sDiff should also be defined


[miDiff]
number
difference in minutes, negative value is acceptable; optional, but if defined, hDiff and sDiff should also be defined


[sDiff]
number
difference in seconds, negative value is acceptable; optional, but if defined, hDiff and miDiff should also be defined



Example  
console.log(getRelativeDateString(new Date(2018, 1, 2), 0, 2, 0)) // '2018-04-02'
console.log(getRelativeDateString(new Date(2018, 1, 2), 0, -2, 0)) // '2017-12-02'


© 2018 Yakima Teng @ https://github.com/Yakima-Teng

string/fillLeft.html.title: fillLeft
string/fillLeft.html.body:
fillLeft(val, len, symbol) ⇒ string
fill a string or number to specified length with specified symbol from left on
Kind: global functionReturns: string - string after filling  



Param
Type
Description




val
string \
number



len
number
target length after filling


symbol
string
used to fill string/number



Example  
console.log(fillLeft('a', 2, '$')) // '$a'
console.log(fillLeft('aa', 2, '$')) // 'aa'
console.log(fillLeft('aaa', 2, '$')) // 'aaa'
console.log(fillLeft('aa', 10, '0') // '00000000aa'


© 2018 Yakima Teng @ https://github.com/Yakima-Teng

Description

number

Alternative method to native Array.prototype.map

Param
Type
Description

Array.

handler
function
function used to handler each element in the array



Example

const handler = item => item + 1

Alternative method to native Array.prototype.filter
Kind: global functionReturns: Array. - a new array  



Param
Type
Description




arr
Array.
an array


test
function
function used to determine whether element in the array should be kept or removed, will be kept if returns true



Example  
const arr = [1, 2, 3, 4]
const test = item => item % 2 === 0
console.log(filter(arr, test)) // [2, 4]

array/findIndex.html.title: findIndex
array/findIndex.html.body:
findIndex(arr, test, [fromIndex]) ⇒ number
Return the index of the first element pass the test function
Kind: global function  



Param
Type
Description




arr
Array.
array


test
function
function used to test array element


[fromIndex]
number
optional, if specified, the search will start at the specified index, negative value is also supported



Example  
const arr = ['1', '2', 'c']
const test = /^[0-9]$/.test
console.log(findIndex(arr, test, 0)) // 0
console.log(findIndex(arr, test, 2)) // -1


© 2018 Yakima Teng @ https://github.com/Yakima-Teng

Return the index of the first element pass the test function
Kind: global function  



Param
Type
Description




arr
Array.
array


test

function used to test array element


[fromIndex]
number
optional, if specified, the search will start at the specified index, negative value is also supported



Example

const test = /^[0-9]$/.test

array/indexOf.html.title: indexOf
array/indexOf.html.body:
indexOf(arr, value, [fromIndex]) ⇒ number
Return the first index of the matched value, -1 if nothing matches

comparison are done after handling with JSON.stringify
if fromIndex is negative, it's counted from the end

Kind: global function  



Param
Type
Description




arr
Array.
the array


value
any



[fromIndex]
number
optional, if specified, the search will start at the specified index, negative value is also supported



Example  
const arr = [1, '2', 'c', '2']
console.log(indexOf(arr, '2')) // 1
console.log(indexOf(arr, '2', 3)) // 3
console.log(indexOf(arr, 'c', 3)) // -1


© 2018 Yakima Teng @ https://github.com/Yakima-Teng

array/lastIndexOf.html.title: lastIndexOf
array/lastIndexOf.html.body:
lastIndexOf(arr, value, [fromIndex]) ⇒ number
Return the last index of the matched value, -1 if nothing matches

comparison are done after handling with JSON.stringify

Kind: global function  



Param
Type
Description




arr
Array.
the array


value
any
the array


[fromIndex]
number
optional, if specified, the search will start at the specified index and from back to front, negative value is also supported



Example  
const arr = [1, '2', 'c', '2']
console.log(lastIndexOf(arr, '2')) // 3
console.log(lastIndexOf(arr, '2', 3)) // 3
console.log(lastIndexOf(arr, '2', 4)) // -1


© 2018 Yakima Teng @ https://github.com/Yakima-Teng

array/contains.html.title: contains
array/contains.html.body:
contains(arr, item, fromIndex) ⇒ boolean
Determine if the array contains a given item (using === after JSON.stringify).
Kind: global function  



Param
Type
Description




arr
Array.
an array


item
any
a given item


fromIndex




Example  
const arr = [1, '2', 'c', { a: '4' }]
console.log(contains(arr, { a: '4' })) // true
console.log(contains(arr, { a: '5' })) // false
console.log(contains(arr, '1')) // false
console.log(contains(arr, '2')) // true


© 2018 Yakima Teng @ https://github.com/Yakima-Teng

Calculate sum of members in an Array

Description




arr
Array.
an array of numbers


numOfDecimalPlaces

number of decimal places to leave; determined automatically if not provided

Example

validatePhone(phone) ⇒ boolean
Judge whether it's a valid phone number

This function is only available for Chinese cars
The regular expression is copied from webpage: http://www.cpic.com.cn/market/qcbx/?hit=ShouyeDhCsQcbx

Kind: global functionReturns: boolean - whether it's a valid phone number  



Param
Type
Description




phone
string
phone number



Example  
console.log(validatePhone('13333333333')) // true
console.log(validatePhone('12333333333')) // false


© 2018 Yakima Teng @ https://github.com/Yakima-Teng

url/goPage.html.title: goPage
url/goPage.html.body:
goPage(path, query)
Go to specified path with specified query parameters
Kind: global function  



Param
Type
Description




path
string
the target absolute path to go to


query
object
the target query parameter in format of object containing key:value pairs



Example  
goPage('http://www.baidu.com', { a: 1, b: 2 })


© 2018 Yakima Teng @ https://github.com/Yakima-Teng

url/serializeParams.html.title: serializeParams
url/serializeParams.html.body:
serializeParams(params) ⇒ string
Serialize params from object to string
Kind: global functionReturns: string - serialized params in string format  



Param
Description




params
an object of key:value pairs



Example  
serializeParams({ a: 3, b: 4 }) => 'a=3&b=4'


© 2018 Yakima Teng @ https://github.com/Yakima-Teng

url/getQueryValue.html.title: getQueryValue
url/getQueryValue.html.body:
getQueryValue(url, key) ⇒ string
Get value of specified query parameter in specified url
Kind: global functionReturns: string - the value of specified query parameter  



Param
Type
Description




url
string
the url, usually got from window.location.href


key

the parameter



Example  
getQueryValue('http://www.baidu.com?a=1&b=c', 'b') // 'c'
getQueryValue('http://www.baidu.com?a=1&b=c', 'c') // ''


© 2018 Yakima Teng @ https://github.com/Yakima-Teng

device/isIOS.html.title: isIOS
device/isIOS.html.body:
isIOS() ⇒ boolean
Judge whether the OS of current device is iOS
Kind: global functionReturns: boolean - whether the OS of current device is iOSExample  
console.log(isIOS()) // true or false

Kind: global functionReturns: object | null - value of localStorage item

storage/setCookie.html.title: setCookie
storage/setCookie.html.body:
setCookie(name, val, expireDays)
Set cookie
Kind: global function  



Param
Description




name
the key/name of the cookie item


val
the value of the cookit item


expireDays
[optional] if set, the cookie item will be outdated after the specified number of days



Example  
setCookie('key', 'value')
setCookie('key', 'value', 5)

storage/setLocalStorage.html.title: setLocalStorage
storage/setLocalStorage.html.body:

Save a key:value pair in localStorage

the value should itself be an object of key:value pairs

Kind: global function  



Param
Type
Description




key
string
the key of the storage item


val
object
the value of the storage item



Example  
setLocalStorage('localStorageItemName', { a: 1, b: '2' })

val

wechat/getWechatRedirectUrl.html.title: getWechatRedirectUrl
wechat/getWechatRedirectUrl.html.body:
getWechatRedirectUrl(appId, targetUrl) ⇒ string
Return Wechat redirect Url where Wechat will pass code query parameter to us
Kind: global function  



Param
Type
Description




appId
string
appId for the Wechat account


targetUrl
string
entire url including the preceding http or https



Example  
console.log(getWechatRedirectUrl('test', 'http://www.baidu.com?a=b')) // 'https://open.weixin.qq.com/connect/oauth2/authorize?appid=test&redirect_uri=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.baidu.com%3Fa%3Db&response_type=code&scope=snsapi_base&state=1#wechat_redirect'


© 2018 Yakima Teng @ https://github.com/Yakima-Teng
